There are no explicit temporal conditions (before/after events or specific triggers), so the intent is for this property to always hold while the system is operating. Therefore, the most straightforward and faithful structured NL is 'always satisfy (measureTV & displayTV)'. This ensures that at all times, both the measurement and the display of tidal volume are continuously satisfied, thus meeting the stated requirement.

explanation: The requirement states that for each breathing cycle, the system shall (must) measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered per cycle). While the atomic propositions available include 'measureTV' (measuring tidal volume) and 'displayTV' (displaying tidal volume), there is no singular atomic proposition covering both actions simultaneously. Therefore, we use the conjunction 'measureTV & displayTV' to capture both intents: measurement and display of tidal volume. Since it is expected to happen on every breathing cycle, we use the template 'whenever breathingCycleStart, within N_DURATION ticks satisfy (measureTV & displayTV)', which stipulates that for every beginning of a breathing cycle, within some short duration (here assumed to be immediately or within a minimal time frame), the system should measure and display tidal volume. This captures the intent of the requirement in a structured, template-driven manner using the available atomic propositions.
